questions.q: Planned headcount by function (production, admin, sales). Any hiring ramp?
Capacity utilization target and shift assumptions.
Planned capex (equipment, fit-out, IT). Timing and amounts.
Depreciation method and useful lives.
Revenue growth target (% or absolute). Volume vs price mix.
Key cost drivers: materials, labor, energy. Any known price changes?
Planned headcount: engineering, sales, marketing, G&A. Hiring plan by quarter?
Average fully-loaded cost per head by function.
Infrastructure, tooling, office. Any large one-off spends?
ARR/MRR growth target. New vs expansion vs churn assumptions.
CAC and payback period targets.
Billable vs non-billable headcount. Utilization target.
Average bill rate and cost per consultant.
Office, software, equipment. Any major investments?
Revenue growth target. Pipeline and win-rate assumptions.
Gross margin and overhead ratio targets.
Warehouse, sales, admin headcount. Seasonal peaks?
Volume and throughput assumptions.
Warehouse, vehicles, systems. Capex by period.
Revenue growth. Category mix and margin targets.
Inventory and working capital assumptions.
Target utilization rate for consulting staff (%). Seasonal variation?
Billable headcount by level (junior, senior, principal). Hiring plan?
Average bill rates by level. Expected rate increases?
Revenue split by engagement type (strategy, implementation, advisory). Changes expected?
Average project size and duration. Pipeline assumptions?
Non-billable overhead: recruiting, training, BD. Expected changes?
Office, travel, technology costs. Remote vs on-site mix?
Target billable hours per attorney (associates vs partners). Realization rate?
Average billing rates by level. Planned rate adjustments?
Collection rate and average days to collect?
Revenue breakdown by practice area (litigation, corporate, IP, etc.).
Associate-to-partner ratio. Lateral hires or promotions planned?
Paralegal and support staff headcount. Outsourcing plans?
Office lease, legal research tools, malpractice insurance costs?
Marketing, business development, and pro-bono allocation?
Engineering headcount by role (frontend, backend, QA, DevOps). Hiring ramp?
Sprint capacity and velocity assumptions. Utilization target?
Contractor vs full-time mix. Nearshore/offshore plans?
Revenue by engagement model (fixed-price, T&M, retainer). Mix changes?
Average project size and backlog depth. Pipeline conversion rate?
Cloud infrastructure, CI/CD tooling, and licensing costs?
Office, equipment, and general overhead. Remote work policy impact?
Placement volume targets (perm vs contract). Fill rate assumptions?
Average placement fee or bill/pay spread by segment?
Contract renewal rate and average assignment duration?
Active contractor headcount and payroll burden (taxes, benefits, insurance)?
Workers' comp and general liability rates by job category?
Internal recruiter and sales headcount. Productivity targets (placements per recruiter)?
Job-board spend, ATS licensing, office costs?
Primary sourcing currencies and expected FX rates. Hedging strategy?
Tariff rates and duty exposure by product category. Expected policy changes?
Average shipping cost per container/unit. Freight rate assumptions?
Average lead time (order to warehouse). Safety stock policy?
Inventory turns target. Obsolescence and shrinkage rates?
Revenue growth target. Customer concentration and channel mix?
Gross margin target by product line. Pricing strategy?
Number of stores. New openings or closures planned?
Average foot traffic and conversion rate by location. Seasonal patterns?
E-commerce vs in-store revenue split. Channel growth targets?
Inventory turns target. Markdown and shrinkage assumptions?
Average basket size and SKU count. Category mix changes?
Occupancy costs (rent, CAM, utilities) per location?
Store labor model: full-time vs part-time mix, wages, and benefits?
Current backlog value and expected new awards. Win rate on bids?
Revenue recognition method (% completion vs completed contract). Schedule assumptions?
Subcontractor cost as % of project value. Key trade availability?
Materials cost inflation assumptions. Supply chain lead times?
Equipment rental vs owned fleet. Major equipment purchases planned?
G&A headcount (PMs, estimators, admin). Overhead allocation method?
Bonding capacity, insurance, and warranty reserve rates?
Number of crews and crew size. Journeyman vs apprentice mix?
Labor rates (union/non-union), overtime assumptions, and seasonal availability?
Hiring or training plans? Expected wage increases?
Key material costs and price trend assumptions (copper, steel, lumber, etc.)?
Tool and vehicle fleet. Replacement or expansion capex?
Backlog by project type. Average job size and duration?
Markup and margin targets. Warranty and callback reserve?
Patient visit volume by provider. Growth targets?
Average reimbursement rate by CPT code mix. Expected rate changes?
Payer mix (commercial, Medicare, Medicaid, self-pay). Collection rates?
Physician, PA/NP, and clinical support headcount. Compensation models?
Administrative and billing staff. Revenue cycle outsourcing?
Facility lease, medical equipment, and EHR/technology costs?
Malpractice insurance, licensing, and compliance costs?
Facility count and bed/chair/unit capacity. Occupancy or utilization targets?
Revenue per patient-day or per procedure. Volume growth assumptions?
Payer mix and contract renegotiation schedule. Rate change expectations?
Clinical staffing ratios (nurse-to-patient, therapist-to-patient). Compliance minimums?
Agency/travel staff usage. Plans to reduce reliance?
Wage growth assumptions. Retention and turnover rates?
Regulatory compliance costs (accreditation, audits, reporting)?
Capital expenditure plans (facility upgrades, medical equipment, IT systems)?
Fee-based revenue streams (advisory, wealth management, fund management, brokerage)? AUM/AUA projections and fee schedule (bps by tier)?
Net interest income: loan book size, weighted average lending rate, cost of funds? Planned growth in the book?
Other revenue: trading, FX, insurance commissions, placement fees, performance fees? Material items and assumptions?
Front-office headcount (relationship managers, advisors, traders, analysts). Base + variable compensation structure and targets?
Middle and back-office headcount (operations, compliance, risk, IT, admin). Fully loaded cost per head by function?
Compensation-to-revenue ratio target? Expected changes in incentive structures?
Provision for credit losses or expected credit loss (ECL) rate assumptions? Portfolio quality trends and coverage ratios?
Regulatory capital requirements (CET1, leverage, liquidity ratios) and cost of capital? Buffer above minimums?
Compliance, legal, audit, and AML/KYC costs? Known regulatory changes or remediation programs?
Core platform costs (banking system, portfolio management, trading infrastructure, custodian fees)?
Cybersecurity, data analytics, and digital transformation spend? Cloud migration plans?
Office occupancy, professional indemnity insurance, and general operational costs?
Revenue model breakdown: transaction fees (take rate), subscription tiers, interchange, lending spread, FX margin? Fee structure by product?
Monthly active users or merchants? Growth trajectory and GMV/TPV assumptions?
Net revenue retention rate? Expansion revenue from existing customers (upsell, cross-sell)?
CAC by channel (paid, organic, partnerships, referral). Blended CAC and LTV/CAC ratio target?
Monthly churn rate by cohort? Expected improvement trajectory?
Sales and marketing headcount and spend allocation. Channel partnership costs?
Engineering and product headcount ramp plan? Build vs buy decisions on core infrastructure?
Cloud infrastructure, payment processing/gateway, and banking-as-a-service costs?
Third-party API costs (identity verification, credit bureau, data providers)?
Fraud and chargeback loss rate assumptions? Risk management headcount, tools, and insurance?
Regulatory licensing costs by jurisdiction? Capital adequacy or e-money safeguarding requirements?
Legal, audit, and ongoing compliance monitoring costs?
